description: You are a radio astronomer at the Robert C. Byrd Green Bank Telescope (GBT) in West Virginia planning a flux calibration observation run for January 20, 2024, at 05:00:00 UTC. Set the observation location to Green Bank Observatory (38.4330° N, 79.8398° W; coordinates are also available in /home/ga/data/observatory_locations.json). Configure the display for radio pointing by disabling the atmosphere and ground, and enabling the equatorial coordinate grid and deep-sky object labels. Capture screenshots of the three standard radio flux calibrators—M1 (Crab Nebula), M42 (Orion Nebula), and M87 (Virgo A)—centered in the view to /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium/. Ensure your environment settings are saved. Write a calibration observation plan containing the source names and the observation date to /home/ga/Desktop/calibration_plan.txt.
